findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "check_claim" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Verify a proposed answer against memory before committing to it. Pass your candidate answer as claim_text. Returns supporting and contradicting evidence with co…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "list_external_services" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: List registered external services available to the calling agent. Returns service IDs, display names, allowed methods/paths, and rate limit state. Does not retu…

description: Dismiss a pending or quarantined skill submission. The skill is marked as dismissed and will not be promoted.

description.name: retract_skill
description.description: Retract a previously promoted skill. Sets the Engine artifact's living status to 'retracted', removing it from future retrieval results. Use when a skill is found to be incorrect or outdated.

description.name: session_debrief
description.description: Structured session-end reflection. Routes discoveries to appropriate capture tools (suggest_constraint, submit_skill, flag_for_review). Produces a receipted debrief record. Call before closing any session longer than 10 minutes.

description.name: get_domain_changelog
description.description: Cross-artefact-type changelog for specified domains since a given timestamp. Returns constraints added/updated, knowledge changes, decisions recorded, and alerts raised/resolved. Use at session start to learn what changed in your domain since your last session.

description.name: get_my_tasks
description.description: Read-through to PlanCrux task graph. Returns tasks assigned to or relevant to the calling agent, filtered by status and priority. Includes stage progress, blockers, and linked MemoryCrux artefact counts.

description.name: get_task_context
description.description: Full task context: task metadata, stages with status and weight, active blockers, linked artefacts (constraints, decisions, knowledge), recent log entries, and pinned master plan version. Assembles the full picture from PlanCrux and MemoryCrux.

description.name: log_progress
description.description: Receipted write-through to PlanCrux's log endpoint. Appends a structured log entry to a task with optional evidence references and stage binding. Cannot change task or stage status (human-only), but records work done, findings, and blockers encountered.

description.name: register_external_service
description.description: Register an external service and store its credential via Vault Transit. Human-only (admin or owner role). The credential is encrypted immediately on receipt and never stored in plaintext. Returns the service registration record without the credential.

description.name: request_credentialed_call
description.description: Proxied external API call with server-side credential injection. VaultCrux retrieves the credential from Vault Transit, injects it per the service's auth template, makes the call, and returns the response. The credential never appears in your context window. Request and response are receipted and au

description.name: list_external_services
description.description: List registered external services available to the calling agent. Returns service IDs, display names, allowed methods/paths, and rate limit state. Does not return credentials or credential references.

description.name: enumerate_memory_facts
description.description: Deterministic fact-table extraction for aggregation questions and curated ESI lookups. Returns a structured row set (subject, predicate, object, date, session_id, confidence) instead of prose. Use this for 'how many', 'total', 'list all' questions — count the rows instead of hoping the LLM enumerate

description.name: build_timeline
description.description: Deterministic timeline constructor for temporal reasoning. Finds all dated events matching your query, normalizes dates, and returns them sorted chronologically. Use for 'what order', 'before/after', 'earliest/latest' questions. Returns unresolved events (found but no date) separately.

description.name: expand_hit_context
description.description: Session-neighborhood expansion around promising retrieval hits. When you find a relevant chunk but the specific fact (name, date, amount) is in a nearby turn, use this to fetch ±N turns from the same session. Recovers facts like 'my parents', '$6', or 'Disney+' that are near but not in the retrieved

description.name: assess_answerability
description.description: Sufficiency gate — can this question be answered with current evidence? Pass your query and optionally the fact rows you have gathered. Returns: answerable (yes/no), missing fields, contradictory fields, recommended next tool, and confidence. Use this BEFORE forcing a best-guess answer. If answerabl

description.name: derive_from_facts
description.description: Safe math and selection over a fact row set. Operations: sum, count, difference, max, min, latest, earliest. Pass the rows from enumerate_memory_facts and get a deterministic result with a computation trace. Removes arithmetic slop from totals, comparisons, and 'which is highest' questions.

description.name: extract_facts_from_sessions
description.description: Scan the tenant's seeded sessions with rule-based extractors (money, counts, dates, project-role, acquire, version-chain) and emit structured facts to the projection stream so they become queryable via enumerate_memory_facts. Use when enumerate_memory_facts returns insufficient rows for aggregation,

description.name: compare_versions
description.description: Temporal-ordered view of all values recorded for an (entity, predicate) pair, with the most-recent-before-as_of flagged as current. Use this when the agent sees contradictory facts ('Rachel moved to Chicago' AND 'Rachel moved to the suburbs') and needs to know which is current. Example calls: {entit

description.name: get_session_dossiers
description.description: Retrieve pre-synthesized per-session memory dossiers (typed: experience | fact | preference; with When/Involving/To-purpose metadata). Use for multi-session or preference-style questions where stitching across conversations is the bottleneck — the dossier already summarises each session's key events

description.name: investigate_question
description.description: Composite server-side investigation tool. Pass a question and the server automatically: (1) detects intent (aggregation/temporal/ordering/knowledge-update/recall), (2) queries the entity index for structured facts, (3) builds a timeline for temporal questions, (4) retrieves memory chunks with the ri

description.name: submit_correction
description.description: Submit a correction for a knowledge item with evidence chain. The original item is never mutated — a versioned enrichment layer is created.

description.name: get_enrichment_status
description.description: Check the status of submitted corrections (pending, corroborated, merged, retracted, expired).

description.name: get_knowledge_gaps
description.description: List gap receipts (coverage + enumeration) for the tenant, filterable by topic and recency.

description.name: fill_gap
description.description: Fill a previously reported gap with new knowledge. Gap must have been reported by a different tenant for cross-tenant credit.

description.name: declare_available_models
description.description: Declare which models are available in this session for orchestration routing. Called once at session start.

description.name: get_escalation_recommendation
description.description: Get model routing recommendation for a query based on composite confidence and difficulty profile. Returns escalation advice: none, recommended, or required.

description.name: get_incident_context
description.description: What went wrong last time we touched this module? Returns past incidents, deploy failures, gotchas, and active constraints for a module or system. Use BEFORE modifying infrastructure code, deploy scripts, or any module with a history of fragility. Surfaces the kind of tribal knowledge that prevents

description.name: get_architecture_rationale
description.description: Why is this module built this way? Aggregates all architectural decisions, active constraints, corrections, and skills for a domain into a coherent narrative. Use BEFORE refactoring or questioning a design choice — the answer is often 'it's that way because of compliance/performance/incident X'. Ret

description.name: check_deploy_readiness
description.description: Is it safe to deploy these changes? Cross-references your changed modules against active constraints, recent incidents, knowledge freshness, and active alerts. Returns a composite verdict (ready/caution/block) with per-module breakdown and actionable recommendations. Use BEFORE deploying to catch co

description.name: get_operator_profile
description.description: Retrieve the human operator's working context: role, domain expertise, communication preferences, quality bar, trusted/distrusted sources. Returns the latest version of the profile. Defaults to the operator linked to the calling agent's passport.

description.name: update_operator_profile
description.description: Update the operator profile. Creates a new version with provenance receipt (does not mutate). Accepts partial updates — fields not provided are preserved from the previous version.

description.name: run_elicitation
description.description: Start or resume a structured elicitation session for a specific knowledge layer. Layers: operating_rhythm, recurring_decisions, dependencies, friction, judgment_patterns, quality_standards, or 'full' for all layers. Returns the first question and session metadata. Use answer_elicitation to submit an

description.name: answer_elicitation
description.description: Submit an answer to an elicitation question. Triggers coverage re-assessment, follow-up question selection, and automatic output routing to appropriate record types (operating rhythms, constraints, decision context, operator profile). Returns the next question or session completion.

description.name: get_operating_rhythms
description.description: Retrieve operating rhythm records for an operator — temporal behavioural patterns capturing what the human actually does (not what their calendar says). Filterable by cadence (daily, weekly, monthly) and delegation status. Returns structured sequences with delegation readiness signals.

description.name: assess_delegation_readiness
description.description: Multi-dimensional delegation readiness assessment. Checks operator profile completeness, operating rhythm coverage, constraint coverage, knowledge coverage, and decision framework coverage relative to the task. Returns overall readiness signal (ready/likely_ready/needs_work/not_ready), dimension-lev

description.name: provision_agent_context
description.description: Generate a structured, receipted context package for a new agent — the equivalent of soul.md + heartbeat.md + user.md but machine-queryable and backed by MemoryCrux records. Includes identity, operator context, active constraints, operating rhythms, knowledge briefing, and heartbeat schedule. Scope:

description.name: decompose_expertise
description.description: Decompose a high-level task class (e.g. 'handle marketing', 'review contracts') into specific steps with knowledge requirements, judgment requirements, delegation difficulty (trivial/moderate/hard/expert_only), existing coverage, and elicitation signals. Identifies which steps can be immediately del

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "register_external_service" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Register an external service and store its credential via Vault Transit. Human-only (admin or owner role). The credential is encrypted immediately on receipt an…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_signals_feed · Private data: memory_claim_check, record_decision_context, checkpoint_decision_state, admit_character_kernel_entry, sync_push, submit_skill, session_debrief, log_progress, register_external_service, request_credentialed_call, list_external_services, get_incident_context, answer_elicitation, get_operating_rhythms, provision_agent_context · Public sink: submit_skill, submit_correction, run_elicitation, answer_elicitation · Destructive: assess_character_drift, extract_facts_from_sessions, run_elicitation.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_signals_feed · Private data: memory_claim_check, record_decision_context, checkpoint_decision_state, admit_character_kernel_entry, sync_push, submit_skill, session_debrief, log_progress, register_external_service, request_credentialed_call, list_external_services, get_incident_context, answer_elicitation, get_operating_rhythms, provision_agent_context · Public sink: submit_skill, submit_correction, run_elicitation, answer_elicitation · Destructive: assess_character_drift, extract_facts_from_sessions, run_elicitation

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
